automation_details: An auto insurance carrier needs to handle bodily-injury claims that span 4-12 weeks. After first notice of loss, an adjuster gathers police reports, medical records, and witness statements; a field inspector evaluates vehicle damage; legal review is triggered above $50k; cases frequently re-enter the evidence-gathering stage when claimants submit late documents; SLAs are tracked per stage with escalation to a supervisor when at risk.

Every weekday at 6am, pull the prior day's transaction file from the SFTP, validate it against a schema, post each row to the GL via the ERP REST API, then email a one-page summary to the controller. The same eight steps run every single day with no human review.

A bank's loan origination workflow takes 2-3 weeks and moves applications through Application Intake, Document Verification, Credit Underwriting, Compliance Review, and Disbursement stages. Underwriters can send applications back to Document Verification when income proofs are insufficient. Each stage has its own SLA, role-based access (loan officer vs underwriter vs compliance analyst), and escalation rules.

When a new employee is created in Workday, automatically provision their accounts: create an Active Directory user, assign to the standard 'Employee' group, create an O365 mailbox, send Slack invite, and order a building badge via the facilities API. Steps run in fixed order; failures retry up to 3 times then alert IT.

A regulator-mandated KYC remediation program where flagged customers must resubmit identity documents, attestations, and beneficial-owner disclosures over 30-90 days. A compliance analyst opens an investigation, may request additional documents multiple times, can escalate to enhanced due diligence, and must produce a full audit trail of every decision for the regulator. Some customers withdraw mid-process.

Hourly sync of product inventory levels from the warehouse management system to the e-commerce storefront. Read deltas since the last successful run, transform to the storefront's schema, push via API, log results. Same script runs 24 times a day deterministically.

A vendor onboarding program where new suppliers go through Legal contract review, Security questionnaire, Finance banking-details verification, and Procurement catalog setup. High-risk vendors trigger an additional InfoSec deep-dive stage. Different teams own different stages, items frequently bounce back to Legal for redlines, and the whole process can take 6-10 weeks.

Three-way match for AP invoices: when an invoice arrives in the inbox, OCR the PDF, look up the PO and goods receipt in SAP, compare quantities and amounts within tolerance, and either post the invoice for payment or route to a single AP clerk. Predictable rules, no stages, completes within minutes.

A telco customer-complaint resolution flow with tiered escalation: Tier 1 attempts a fix, the case can come back if the fix doesn't hold within 14 days, Tier 2 does deeper troubleshooting, and Tier 3 may issue credits or coordinate field dispatch. Cases carry SLAs, the customer can add comments any time, and supervisors can create ad-hoc tasks for follow-ups.

Every Monday at 8am, query the data warehouse for last week's KPIs, render a fixed PowerPoint template, save to SharePoint, and email a link to the leadership distribution list. Pure schedule-driven report generation.

A credit-card chargeback dispute process where the merchant and cardholder exchange evidence over 45-75 days through pre-arbitration and arbitration phases. The investigator gathers receipts, signatures, and shipping proof; can re-request evidence; and a card-network ruling may force the case back to an earlier evidence stage. Every interaction must be logged for network audits.

When a lead-form is submitted on the marketing website, validate the email, enrich with Clearbit, create a Lead record in Salesforce with the right owner based on territory rules, and send a templated welcome email. Runs end-to-end in under 30 seconds with no human in the loop.

A health-plan prior-authorization workflow where requests move through Intake, Clinical Review, Medical-Director Review (only for denials or high-cost requests), and either Approval or Appeals. Appeals can re-open a closed case and route it back to Clinical Review. Multiple clinician roles, strict turnaround SLAs imposed by CMS, and full audit trail required.

A nightly database maintenance job: take a full backup, validate the backup with a checksum, copy to S3 Glacier, prune backups older than 90 days, and post a success/failure message to the #ops Slack channel. Identical steps every night.

A property and casualty claim handling experience where the adjuster manages a long-running situation involving the policyholder, a public adjuster, contractors, and an SIU fraud investigator when red flags appear. Claims pause while waiting on contractor estimates, resume on receipt, and may re-enter Investigation if new info contradicts earlier findings. Stage-aware permissions ensure the SIU stage is only visible to fraud investigators.

A user clicks 'Reset password' in the customer portal: verify the email exists, generate a single-use token, store a hash with a 15-minute TTL, and send a templated reset email. Sub-second runtime, no exceptions, no human touch.

Public-sector child-welfare investigations that span weeks to months: an intake worker opens a referral, a caseworker conducts home visits and interviews, supervisors approve safety plans, and the case may transition to court-ordered services or be closed. Caseworkers frequently add ad-hoc tasks (additional collateral interviews, school visits) and the case re-enters Investigation when new allegations come in.

Order placement on an e-commerce checkout: validate cart, check inventory, authorize payment via Stripe, create the order in OMS, send a confirmation email, and emit a shipment-create event. Five steps, fixed order, runs in seconds, predictable error handling on each step.

A commercial mortgage underwriting program where deals move through Origination, Property Appraisal, Environmental Review, Credit Committee, Legal Documentation, and Closing. Deals frequently bounce between Appraisal and Credit Committee, environmental issues can pause the case, and each stage has distinct roles (analyst, appraiser, attorney, closer) with stage-restricted views.

Every 5 minutes, poll a queue for new IoT sensor readings, run them through an anomaly-detection model, and write any anomalies to a time-series database with a tag indicating the rule that fired. No state, no review, no humans.

An HR misconduct-investigation workflow that brings together HR Business Partners, Legal, and Ethics & Compliance. The investigator interviews witnesses, collects evidence, may pause for legal hold, and produces a finding that can route to Discipline, Mediation, or Closure-No-Action. Re-entry happens when new complaints surface against the same subject. Strict access controls and audit trail driven by employment law.

Renewal billing for a SaaS subscription: 7 days before renewal, attempt to charge the saved card, on success extend the subscription and email a receipt, on failure send a dunning email and retry on day 3 and day 7 before downgrading to the free tier. Deterministic ladder.

A municipal building-permit review process spanning 6-12 weeks: applications route through Zoning, Building, Fire, Health, and Public Works departments. Any reviewer can issue a correction notice that sends the application back to the applicant; an inspector schedules site visits; the planning commission may need to convene; and the applicant can request hearings. Cross-departmental coordination with stage-aware visibility.

Daily reconciliation between Stripe payouts and the bank statement: download both files, match by amount and reference, write matched and unmatched rows to two CSV outputs, and email finance the unmatched file. Deterministic file-to-file ETL with no branching beyond match/unmatch.

An order-fulfillment exception management process for a retailer: when a shipment is partially fulfilled, backordered, lost in transit, or returned damaged, a coordinator opens an exception case to investigate, contact the carrier, communicate with the customer, optionally issue replacements or refunds, and close out. Cases can take days to weeks, involve multiple systems (WMS, TMS, OMS), and frequently spawn ad-hoc tasks like 'verify with warehouse manager'.

Pharmaceutical adverse event case management: when a possible drug side-effect is reported by a patient, physician, or call center, a pharmacovigilance specialist opens a case, gathers source documents, requests follow-up info from the reporter (often multiple rounds), the medical reviewer assesses causality, and regulatory affairs files reports with FDA and EMA on strict timelines. Cases can re-open if new info arrives months later. Audit trail is mandated by 21 CFR Part 11.

Hourly ETL job that extracts incremental rows from the CRM (where modified_at > last watermark), transforms them with a fixed mapping, and loads into the Snowflake warehouse. Watermark is updated atomically on success. Same job runs every hour, no humans involved.

Workers' compensation claim handling that runs 30 days to multiple years: an adjuster opens a claim after a workplace injury, schedules independent medical exams, coordinates light-duty return-to-work programs, manages indemnity and medical payments, and may litigate disputed claims with assigned defense counsel. Claims re-open when symptoms recur. Multiple roles (adjuster, nurse case manager, attorney) act in stage-restricted views.

Bot for ticket auto-close: every night, find ServiceNow tickets in 'Resolved' state with no customer activity for 7 days, set them to 'Closed', and email the requester a templated closure note. Pure rule-based maintenance job.

Higher-education admissions appeals process where rejected applicants can submit an appeal package; an admissions officer reviews, may request additional materials (transcripts, recommendations, personal statements), routes to a faculty committee for borderline cases, and the committee may overturn or uphold. Appeals can be re-opened if new test scores arrive. Stage-aware visibility — applicants only see their own cases.

Sales tax calculation for an e-commerce checkout: given cart contents and ship-to address, call Avalara, apply the returned tax amount to the order total, and persist the tax record for later reporting. Sub-second deterministic call inside the order flow.

Real estate residential closing/escrow that runs 30-45 days from contract to keys: an escrow officer coordinates title search, inspections, appraisal, lender funding conditions, and seller disclosures. The deal frequently bounces back to inspection or appraisal when issues surface, the closing date is rescheduled multiple times, and buyer/seller/lender/agent each have stage-restricted access. Audit trail required by RESPA.

Daily currency-rate sync: at 7am UTC pull the ECB reference rates feed, normalize to a fixed schema, upsert into the rates table in the ERP, and emit a Kafka event so downstream services can refresh caches. Identical 4-step pipeline every day.

M&A due diligence project that runs 60-180 days: working teams in legal, finance, tax, IT, HR, and commercial each review documents in a virtual data room, raise findings, request additional documents from the seller, and converge on red-flag and value-driver memos. Findings re-open earlier work streams when new docs arrive. Strict access controls, full audit trail, and a steering committee approves stage transitions.

Auto-approval routing for travel expense reports under $500: when a report is submitted in Concur and all line items are below the policy thresholds and the total is under $500, auto-approve and post to GL; otherwise route to the manager. No stages, single deterministic decision.

Asylum application processing that spans months to years: an asylum officer collects affidavits and country-condition evidence, schedules interviews (sometimes multiple), may refer to immigration court, the case can pause for years pending appeals, and re-enters review on remand. Multiple government roles with strict separation of duties and full audit trail mandated by statute.

Loyalty points accrual after a purchase: when an order moves to 'shipped', look up the customer's tier, calculate points using the tier multiplier, add them to the loyalty account, and email the customer a confirmation. Runs once per order, deterministic, sub-second.

Patent prosecution before the USPTO: an in-house patent attorney files an application, responds to office actions over 2-5 years (each requiring claim amendments and arguments), conducts examiner interviews, and may appeal to PTAB. Each office action re-opens the claim-drafting work, deadlines are statutory and trigger escalations, and external counsel may join for appeals.
Webhook handler for Shopify order-paid events: receive the webhook, validate the HMAC, look up the SKU in the WMS, create a pick-pack-ship task, and acknowledge. Stateless, sub-second, fixed flow.

Construction project change-order management on multi-month builds: when the GC, owner, or architect requests a scope change, the project manager opens a change-order case, gathers cost and schedule impacts from subs, the architect reviews for design fit, the owner approves or rejects, and approved changes feed back into project planning. Cases can re-enter pricing when subs revise, and disputes escalate to mediation.

User offboarding when an HR termination event fires: revoke Active Directory account, remove from all groups, disable O365, deactivate Slack, retrieve laptop via the IT ticketing system, and log the audit record. Fixed 6-step sequence, no judgment calls.

Insurance subrogation recovery process: after a paid first-party claim, a subrogation specialist opens a recovery case, identifies at-fault third parties, sends demand letters, negotiates with the third party's carrier or files arbitration, and may litigate. Cases run 6-24 months, can pause pending arbitration, and re-enter negotiation when offers change. Multiple specialists act under strict accounting controls.

CI/CD deployment pipeline: on git push to main, run unit tests, build a Docker image, push to ECR, deploy to staging, run smoke tests, and on success promote to production. Same pipeline runs on every commit, deterministic, fully automated.

Clinical trial patient enrollment that runs over weeks per patient: a coordinator screens potential participants, the principal investigator confirms eligibility, the patient goes through informed consent (often re-explained over multiple visits), baseline assessments are scheduled, and the patient may withdraw at any point. Strict regulatory audit trail (GCP/ICH-E6), IRB notifications, and stage-restricted access for blinded studies.

Server health check routine: every minute, ping a fleet of 50 servers, check CPU/memory/disk thresholds, restart any service flagged unhealthy via systemd, and post anomalies to PagerDuty. Stateless, deterministic, no humans.

Tax audit response handling for an enterprise: when the IRS opens an examination, the tax department coordinates document collection across business units, responds to multiple rounds of IDRs (Information Document Requests) over 12-36 months, may protest findings to Appeals, and could ultimately litigate in Tax Court. Each IDR can re-open prior workstreams, and access is restricted by entity and topic.

Email-to-ticket automation: poll the support inbox every 2 minutes, parse new emails, classify with a fixed regex into one of 5 queues, create a Zendesk ticket via API with the email body as the description, and send an auto-acknowledgement. Linear, deterministic, no judgment.

Medical malpractice claim investigation that runs 1-3 years: a claims specialist opens a case after a notice of intent to sue, retains defense counsel, gathers medical records, coordinates expert reviews, sets reserves, and progresses through pre-suit, discovery, mediation, and possibly trial stages. Cases re-enter discovery when new experts are added, and reserves are revised at each stage gate. Strict separation of duties between claims, legal, and finance.

Generate and email invoice PDF: when an invoice is finalized in NetSuite, render a PDF from the standard template, attach it to a templated email to the customer's AR contact, and log the send in the customer's record. One-shot, sub-second, deterministic.

Government grant application review for federally funded research: program officers receive applications, route them to peer-review panels, panels score and re-score across multiple meetings, the council recommends funding, and applicants can request reconsideration. The review can re-enter peer-review when the council requests additional input, deadlines are statutory, and conflict-of-interest screening gates each stage.

B2B SaaS customer onboarding that runs 4-8 weeks per new account: a Customer Success Manager kicks off with a discovery call, the technical integration team configures SSO and API connections, data migration runs in stages with the customer validating each batch, training sessions are scheduled around the customer's availability, and go-live happens once acceptance criteria are signed off. The program frequently pauses for the customer's IT team and re-enters the migration stage when sample-data validation fails. CSM, solutions architect, and data engineer all have stage-restricted views.

New employee onboarding that runs the day before start date through end of week 1: provision Active Directory and email, ship the laptop, enroll in payroll, assign the standard new-hire training playlist, and (only for engineers) add to the GitHub org and the on-call rotation. The branch on role is a single deterministic if-statement; there is no rework, no SLA escalation, and no human judgment beyond the initial role flag.

GDPR data-subject access request fulfillment with a hard 30-day regulatory clock: receive the request via the privacy portal, verify the requester's identity against IDP records, fan out searches to 12 source systems, dedupe and redact third-party PII, package the export, and deliver via secure download link. Always the same six steps, no humans in the loop except a single privacy-officer review at the end.

Annual employee performance review cycle running 8-10 weeks: employees write a self-review, peers submit feedback, managers draft assessments, calibration meetings adjust ratings across teams, HR reviews for bias and consistency, and final ratings drive compensation decisions. Drafts often re-enter calibration when comp envelopes change, and stage-aware visibility ensures employees never see their own calibration discussions.

Tier-1 help-desk ticket handling for password resets and software-install requests: classify the ticket via keyword rules, attempt the standard remediation script, on success email the user and close, on failure escalate to Tier-2 by reassigning the queue. 95% of tickets close in under 15 minutes with a fixed step list.

Customer refund request routing: under $100 auto-approve and issue via Stripe; $100-$1000 route to the support manager for a single approval click; over $1000 route to the regional director. Single rule-based decision tree, no stages, no re-entry, completes the same business day.

Strategic procurement for IT software over $250k spanning 8-14 weeks: a sourcing analyst opens an evaluation, runs an RFP, security architecture conducts a deep review, legal negotiates the MSA and DPA in multiple rounds, finance models TCO, and a steering committee approves at gate reviews. Vendors are frequently sent back for revised proposals after security findings, and the case can pause indefinitely while waiting on a vendor counter-proposal.

Marketing campaign approval for a major product launch: a brief moves through creative concept, copy review, brand standards, legal disclosures, executive sign-off, and translation. Each reviewer can send it back with comments, and creative iterations frequently bounce 3-4 times before reaching launch. The case can take 3-6 weeks per major campaign and different reviewer roles see only their stage.

Software bug lifecycle on the engineering side: a bug is filed in Jira, triaged in the daily standup, assigned to an engineer, fixed in a feature branch, reviewed via pull request, merged when approved, deployed, and closed. Bugs can be reopened if the fix doesn't hold, but the workflow itself is the same fixed Jira state machine with no SLA escalations or stage-aware access.

Production deployment with a manual approval gate: a release candidate is built from main, deployed to staging, smoke-tested, and posted to a Slack approval thread; a release manager clicks approve and the same image is promoted to production with canary then full rollout. One human gate inside an otherwise deterministic pipeline that runs in under 30 minutes end to end.

Litigation hold management for active and threatened cases: when in-house legal issues a hold notice, identify all relevant custodians and data sources, preserve mailboxes and shared drives, send custodian acknowledgments and track responses, monitor for departing employees, periodically re-confirm scope as discovery progresses, and release the hold on case resolution. Holds can run 1-5 years, custodian lists shift over time, and full audit trail is mandatory for sanctions defense.

SOC 2 Type II audit response coordination over a 3-month observation window: the GRC team receives auditor evidence requests, routes them to control owners across IT, HR, and engineering, reviews evidence for completeness, responds in the auditor's portal, and addresses follow-up questions and exceptions. Many requests bounce back to control owners for clarification, the audit pauses for sampling cycles, and a final readout drives remediation tasks fed back into next year's program.

Customer-billing complaint resolution: a customer calls about an incorrect charge, a Tier-1 agent investigates with billing operations, may escalate to a senior agent for adjustments above policy limits, and may refer to the dispute team if the customer claims fraud. Resolution can take 1-10 business days, the same complaint can re-open if the customer rejects the adjustment, and SLA breaches escalate to a manager.

Customer-initiated account closure: the customer submits a closure request via the portal, the system verifies identity via MFA, transfers any remaining balance to the linked bank account, closes the account, archives records per the retention policy, and emails confirmation. Five steps, no stages, no humans, completes within minutes.

Engineering incident postmortem program for sev-1 outages: incident commander writes the timeline, contributing service owners draft technical analysis, the reliability team reviews for systemic factors, action items are scoped and assigned to teams, and a final readout goes to engineering leadership. Drafts cycle through reviewers multiple times, action items can re-open the analysis when new evidence surfaces, and the postmortem can take 4-8 weeks to close.

Daily expense-report batch processing: at 7pm pull all submitted reports from Concur, validate against policy rules, auto-approve those fully compliant, batch-post approved reports to the GL, and queue exceptions for the AP analyst's morning queue. Same five-step batch every weekday with no in-flight human interaction.

Morning system-health digest: at 6:30am query 14 monitoring sources (CloudWatch, Datadog, PagerDuty, app-level health endpoints), aggregate into a single dashboard snapshot, render an HTML email, and send to the on-call distribution list. Pure compose-and-send routine that runs identically every day.

Standard ITIL change request through the weekly Change Advisory Board: requestor files an RFC, fills out the impact and rollback templates, the CAB reviews on Tuesdays and approves or rejects, on approval the requestor implements during the next change window, and post-implementation review confirms success. Linear with one approval gate; rejected RFCs are revised and re-submitted as a new ticket rather than re-entering.

New-business commercial auto insurance underwriting on small-fleet accounts: broker submits an application, an underwriter pulls MVRs and loss runs, a rater computes the premium using filed rules, the underwriter approves or declines based on a fixed eligibility matrix, and a quote is issued. Typically completes in 1-3 business days with no stages or re-entry — declines simply close the submission.

Customer churn-save program for at-risk enterprise accounts: when usage signals or NPS flag a risk, the CSM opens a save case, conducts discovery calls with the customer's stakeholders, loops in the executive sponsor and product leadership for a tailored save plan, monitors execution over 60-120 days, and either closes as saved or hands off to the deflection team. Plans frequently pivot when new objections surface and re-enter the discovery stage.

Capital purchase orders over $1M move through requestor, department head, divisional CFO, group CFO, and CEO sign-off. Each level is a single approve-or-reject decision following a written delegation-of-authority matrix. Approvals routinely take 2-3 weeks because executives are frequently traveling and the chain pauses until the current approver acts; rejected POs are killed and the requestor must file a new one rather than amending in place.

Enterprise software license renewals: IT records the upcoming renewal, security re-runs its assessment, finance confirms budget, procurement opens commercial negotiations with the vendor on pricing and terms, legal reviews any changes to the master agreement, and the business owner signs. Negotiations frequently bounce back to security when the vendor changes its data-handling clauses, and the program runs 6-10 weeks per renewal across these teams.

Inpatient hospital discharge coordination: the attending physician medically clears the patient, a discharge planner arranges home health or skilled-nursing placement, pharmacy reconciles take-home medications, social work addresses transportation and insurance authorization, and billing settles the account. The work spans hours to multiple days, can pause when a placement falls through, and frequently requires the planner to revisit clearance after new clinical findings.

Field-service maintenance dispatch for commercial HVAC: when a customer reports a fault, route the work order to the closest qualified technician, the technician travels on-site, runs the standard diagnostic checklist, performs the listed repair if parts are on the truck, and submits the completed work order with photos. 80% of jobs close in a single visit on a fixed step list.

K-12 student disciplinary action for serious incidents: an incident report is filed, an assistant principal interviews witnesses, the student and parents attend a hearing, the principal issues a determination (warning, suspension, expulsion referral), and families may appeal to the superintendent. Findings can re-open if new witnesses come forward, the case carries an audit record required by state education code, and access is restricted by role.

Wedding event coordination on the venue side over 6-12 months per booking: the coordinator confirms the date, collects deposits from contracted vendors (caterer, florist, DJ, photographer), runs a tasting, finalizes the floor plan and timeline, manages day-of execution, and reconciles billing. The plan frequently shifts when vendors fall through or guest counts change, multiple vendors negotiate simultaneously, and the coordinator improvises new tasks as the date approaches.

Annual corporate budgeting cycle running August through November: each department submits a plan in a standard template, FP&A reviews and questions assumptions, executives adjust allocations against strategic priorities, departments revise and resubmit, and the board approves the consolidated budget. Multiple revision rounds bounce plans back to departments, and FP&A pauses departmental work when corporate guidance changes mid-cycle.

Hiring for a Vice President role over 8-16 weeks: the executive sponsor defines the scope, the recruiter sources candidates, candidates progress through phone screens, panel interviews, executive interviews, and reference checks; the recruiter and hiring manager negotiate offer terms, sometimes returning to additional interviews when finalists raise new requirements. Each candidate carries a separate file with stage-restricted notes for the panel and the executive sponsor.

Crisis communications response to a public incident: when an issue breaks, the comms lead opens a response, the legal team reviews messaging, the executive sponsor approves the public statement, social and press teams distribute, and the team monitors reaction and revises follow-on statements. Each new development can re-open earlier drafts, the cadence depends on what reporters and regulators do next, and a full record of approvals is kept for litigation defense.

Standardized engineering hiring pipeline for individual-contributor roles: candidate completes a recruiter screen, a coding assessment, a virtual onsite of four 60-minute interviews, and a debrief; the panel votes hire or no-hire and the recruiter sends an offer or a rejection. Same five steps for every candidate, no revisions, decision is captured in the ATS at the debrief.

Residential real estate sale on the seller's side from listing to close, typically 30-90 days: agent lists the property, hosts showings and open houses, negotiates offers, manages the inspection contingency (which frequently triggers a price renegotiation or repair request), coordinates appraisal and title, and closes at the title company. Inspection findings can send the deal back to negotiation, and the seller, buyer, lender, agents, and title agent all have stage-restricted views of the file.

RFP evaluation for a department-level software purchase: procurement issues the RFP to a pre-qualified vendor list, vendors submit responses by a hard deadline, the evaluation committee scores each response on a fixed rubric, finalists give a 60-minute demo on a scheduled day, and the committee selects the winner in a single decision meeting. The whole evaluation completes in 4 weeks on a fixed calendar.

New-product launch readiness review: the launch lead works a 40-item checklist covering engineering readiness, GTM materials, support enablement, legal sign-off, and pricing setup. Each item has a single named owner; the launch is GO when all 40 items are checked off in the launch tracker. Same checklist used for every launch, no stages or rework loops in the launch tooling itself.

Tenant move-out for a property-management company on routine end-of-lease vacates: tenant gives notice via the portal, the property manager schedules a move-out inspection, the inspector documents condition with photos, the office calculates security-deposit deductions per the standardized schedule, and the system mails the refund or balance-due notice within 21 days. No stages, no negotiation in this routine flow — disputes are handled through a separate process.

Corporate income-tax return preparation for a multi-entity group: the tax team gathers trial balances and book-to-tax adjustments from each entity, prepares federal and state returns in the tax engine, internal review identifies issues that bounce work back to the entity finance teams, an external advisor reviews complex positions, and the returns are e-filed. The cycle runs September through April, frequently pauses for missing source data, and is subject to IRS examination.

Monthly NAV close for a hedge fund: pricing pulls market data at cutoff, accounting books accruals and corporate actions, a senior accountant reviews variance reports, the controller signs off on the NAV, and the administrator releases NAV to investors by T+5. Same fixed sequence every month, exceptions are tracked but resolved within the same close — no re-entry across cycles.

SaaS contract renewal cadence for the standard mid-market segment: at T-90 days the CSM sends a templated outreach, at T-60 reviews usage health on the standard scorecard, at T-30 sends the renewal quote, and at T-0 either books the renewal or routes to the deflection workflow. Same cadence on every account, no executive escalations or save plans in this segment.

Industrial permit-to-work for hot-work in a refinery on a single shift: the requestor identifies the work and hazards, the area authority verifies isolations are in place, the safety officer signs the permit, work proceeds with periodic checks, and the requestor closes out the permit at end of shift. Strict ordering enforced by safety procedure, completes within an 8-hour shift, no re-entry — expired permits must be re-issued as new permits.

International wire-transfer processing with compliance screening: the originating branch enters the wire, the system runs OFAC and sanctions screens, AML rules flag any threshold or pattern hits to the compliance queue, a compliance analyst clears or rejects flagged wires, and cleared wires settle through the correspondent network. Most wires complete in minutes; flagged wires resolve within hours on the same fixed clear-or-reject decision.

Mortgage appraisal review on conventional residential loans: the appraiser submits a report, an internal review appraiser checks comparables and methodology, may issue a revision request that sends the report back to the appraiser, and approves once acceptable. Revisions can cycle 2-3 times when comp selection is contested, the underwriter waits on the cleared appraisal, and the reviewer's judgment on adjustments is the gating factor.
